name: notifications
description: Notification substrate core. Owns the universal delivery record (Notification, NotificationDelivery), the versioned template registry (NotificationTemplate), the per-user preference resolver (NotificationPreference), the module-fed category registry (NotificationCategory), and the digest scheduler (NotificationDigest). Channel-agnostic — provider transports live in sibling modules (notifications-in-app, notifications-mail, notifications-push, notifications-sms). Callers ALWAYS go through the single dispatch entry point (`$user->notify(...)` or `Notifications::dispatch(...)`) which emits an event-carried-state payload to the send queue; channel modules subscribe to that event and translate to their transport. That discipline keeps the door open to extract to a Node microservice later without a rewrite — the same event schema would just point at Redis Streams instead of the local queue.

emails.description: Every module owns its own React Email templates at modules/<name>/emails/*.tsx. Shared React Email components (Layout, Button, Footer, BrandHeader) come from packages/notifications-emails-shared. Renderer walks modules/*/emails/ at CI build time + emits pre-rendered HTML with Blade placeholders. No Node in the PHP runtime.

microserviceReadiness.description: This core is designed so a future extraction to a NestJS microservice is a port, not a rewrite. Five disciplines every consumer honours.
disciplines: Single dispatch entry point (`Notifications::dispatch()`) — no module imports from notifications-mail/-sms/-push/-in-app directly.
Event-carried state — every dispatch payload includes recipient (id, email, phone, name, locale, timezone) + tenant branding (logo, sender, postal_address). No recipient lookups by the receiver.
No cross-module DB reads — consumers subscribe to NotificationDelivered/Failed events + write their own state.
Channel modules are event-driven — respond to NotificationDispatched, emit channel-scoped completion events. Never invoked directly.
OpenAPI spec for every HTTP surface — inbox, preferences, templates, subscriptions. Same spec becomes the NestJS contract on extraction.
triggerConditions: Three or more Laravel apps consume notifications
AI-driven personalization arrives (Node/Python runtime required for ML)

central.description: Public one-click unsubscribe surface. Token-based (signed URL from mail footer). RFC 8058 List-Unsubscribe-Post compliant.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant notification search + delivery diagnostics for support / abuse investigation.

tenant.description: Tenant-scoped inbox + preferences + template admin. Push device management lives in notifications-push. Provider webhooks live in the respective channel module.
